Riverfell Logistics Ltd (ASX: RVF) — Internal Cyber Incident Report — not for external release under ASX Listing Rule 3.1A while confidentiality is maintained.
On 2 June 2026 at 03:40 AEST, a malware event disrupted the FreightHub scheduling module at the Wattle Creek Distribution Centre, causing consignment manifest delays and deferred carrier allocations.
Per Dr. Owen Park (CISO), the intrusion was contained by 07:15 AEST; two application servers were isolated and restored from clean images, with monitoring heightened across all depots.
Preliminary data review indicates exposure of names and business emails of 127 contractor drivers and two internal employee identifiers; no TFNs, bank account details, or health/credit data were accessed.
An assessment under the OAIC Notifiable Data Breaches scheme is underway; current risk is assessed as low to moderate, subject to forensic validation.
If the FreightHub backlog exceeds 72 hours, internal forecast indicates a 6–8% reduction in June revenue for Rail & Intermodal; given RVF’s FY26 guidance range, this could be material to the issuer if sustained.
This information is not generally available; continuous disclosure obligations under LR 3.1 are being monitored, and LR 3.1A applies while confidentiality is maintained and a reasonable person would not expect immediate disclosure.
We will prepare an ASX announcement for release without delay if confidentiality is lost or the impact is assessed as materially price-sensitive; a trading halt request remains an option under ASX Guidance Note 8.
RVF is in a pre-close blackout under the Securities Trading Policy until FY26 results release; staff (including Directors) must not trade and any analyst engagement must be confined to publicly available information.
Customer and supplier communications must avoid selective disclosure; teams may refer to the already lodged ASX announcement dated 24 May 2026 titled “Cybersecurity Uplift Stage 1” and the 2025 Sustainability Report (both generally available).
By contrast, the 2019 routing outage described in the 2020 Annual Report is stale and unrelated to the current event and should not be conflated with this incident.
Two EFT remittance files for contractor payments were queued but not transmitted; no customer orders or financial transactions were altered, and reconciliation controls remain intact.
Note: BlueRange Networks Pty Ltd posted a public blog this morning about a regional fibre cut; while operationally relevant to carriers, it is a non-issuer update and not indicative of RVF’s disclosure status.
Ms. Lila Warrender (CFO) is chairing a daily incident call to reassess issuer-relative materiality; any suspected external leak must be escalated immediately for potential market announcement.
Media enquiries: media@riverfelllogistics.com.au; Investor relations: Ms. Warrender via ir@riverfellogistics.com.au; Security contact: Dr. Park via security@riverfelllogistics.com.au.
